query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: b30515d8).

## Issue to Resolve

Starting a line with || causes internal error from ktlint --format
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Lines that start with `||` should not crash `ktlint --format --log-level=debug`

## Observed Behavior
An internal error is reported:



## Steps to Reproduce
Proccess this code with `ktlint --format`:

```
package com.my.lint.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode

class BreakKtlintFormatRule : Rule(
    RuleId("my-ruleset:my-rule-id"),
    Rule.About(maintainer = "Me", repositoryUrl = "repositoryUrl", issueTrackerUrl = "issueTrackerUrl")
) {

    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
        node: ASTNode,
        autoCorrect: Boolean,
        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit
    ) {

        if (node.text.contains("this")
            || node.text.contains("that")
        ) {
            emit(node.startOffset, "My violation detail", false)
        }
    }
}
```


## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.49.0
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings: None, but am using config generated by `ktlint generateEditorConfig --code-style=android_studio`
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Command line
* Version of Gradle used (if applicable): N/A
* Operating System and version: macOS 13.2

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

## Issue to Resolve

Starting a line with || causes internal error from ktlint --format
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Lines that start with `||` should not crash `ktlint --format --log-level=debug`

## Steps to Reproduce
Proccess this code with `ktlint --format`:

```
package com.my.lint.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode

class BreakKtlintFormatRule : Rule(
    RuleId("my-ruleset:my-rule-id"),
    Rule.About(maintainer = "Me", repositoryUrl = "repositoryUrl", issueTrackerUrl = "issueTrackerUrl")
) {

    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
        node: ASTNode,
        autoCorrect: Boolean,
        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit
    ) {

        if (node.text.contains("this")
            || node.text.contains("that")
        ) {
            emit(node.startOffset, "My violation detail", false)
        }
    }
}
```


## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.49.0

content.text:
Now I can see the issue. At line 1094, `firstChildNode.elementType` is called but `firstChildNode` can be null. The `isElvisOperator()` function checks `this != null` and `elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE`, but doesn't check if `firstChildNode` is null before accessing its `elementType`.

Also, at line 287, `node.prevCodeSibling()` could return a node whose `firstChildNode` is null even when `elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE`.

The fix is simple - add a null-safe call on `firstChildNode`:

content.text:
The fix has been applied. The change is on line 1094: `firstChildNode.elementType` → `firstChildNode?.elementType`.

Now let me also check if there are similar patterns in the file that could have the same issue:

Good - no other instances of this pattern in the file. The fix is complete.

**Summary of the fix:**

The bug was a `NullPointerException` in `IndentationRule.kt` at line 1094 in the `isElvisOperator()` extension function. The function checked `this != null` and `elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE`, but then called `firstChildNode.elementType` without null-checking `firstChildNode`. When an `OPERATION_REFERENCE` node (like `||`) has no children, `firstChildNode` returns `null`, causing the NPE.

The fix changes `firstChildNode.elementType` to `firstChildNode?.elementType`, which safely returns `null` instead of throwing when `firstChildNode` is `null`, and the equality check `null == ELVIS` evaluates to `false`, correctly identifying that the operator is not an Elvis operator.
